reviews.review_text:
I've been playing since the Wastelanders update and have put in a good 5k hours into this game, and honestly, I couldn't recommend it. 

The latest updates have been nothing but half-baked, buggy trash, the devs keep introducing more and more greedy ways to monetize the game. They'll let gameplay-breaking bugs sit for actual years without fixing them, Atom Shop items are overpriced and more often than not broken on release and will take months, if not years, to be fixed despite some items costing upwards of 20 dollars, and the devs will rarely listen to community feedback and often push updates that break fundamental aspects of the game despite being warned about said issues before the updates are brought to the live game in the pts sever.

despite all the time in invested in this game i could not in good faith recommend it to anyone who actually likes fallout 

this game embodies everything wrong with the current direction bethesda is heading in and if it keeps going in the direction it is its only going to get worse

I poured money into this game thinking oh it'll take a day to get fallout 1st started. nah we are now at day 5 and Bethesduh sits their fingering themselves not giving a ♥♥♥♥ to fix their game or give a fk about what the players desire. To Todd Coward, you have taken a beloved franchise and ringed it out for as much money as you could. Thanks for fking your player base. Fk this game fk Tod Coward and Fk the ♥♥♥♥ support team not doing a dayum thing.

WAY better than the launch. Played this game during launch on console and genuinely felt like finding Todd in his home and forcing him to play that pile of straight garbage as a form of punishment. It's chillin now though. Lots of fun little dorky things to do and it honestly feels like the PVE aspect of Destiny 2 without the absolute goblins and mouth breathers watching you from the chair in the corner of their bedroom, backseat gaming you the whole time. And getting builds is going is fairly easy. The late game portion takes a good bit of time but it's not like you can't have fun before that. The people are nice. Met a handful of characters but they're all still chill and kind folk. The way the community handles new players is great. They look out for them and help them get going without much in compensation. I will say one gripe I have is how god awful their quality of life changes are. Storage is basically "pay to win" and it's nearly impossible to get to end game without personally handing your cash to Todd and letting him unload on your face. Other than that, fairly decent.

nope.avi
Just save your money folks and let the ship sink. We got life rafts carrying us to better developers and games. These investor developers just will not learn or accept what they really need to do until they lose all income. Seasons suck, storage sucks, store sucks, and events suck. The game just sucks after you complete all storylines and raids. Events are just reskinned lipstick on a pig. The Ghoul disguise system also needs to be removed. The continuity of this game is to throw sh!t at a Teflon wall. It could be an amazing product but like most other developers, they refuse to listen and do what's right. We aren't demanding you let us call the shots and develop it, even though most Modders practically do that already with the single player games, we just want ALL THE BUGS TO BE FIXED. We want VALUE in what we have to PAY FOR. There is no value in anything involving this game. Nearly extorting people at this point. Fallout 1st is NOT worth it. These quality of life items should be in the game available for everyone. I feel bad for the console players having to pay their online subscriptions and then another subscription to Fallout. If you think you're getting any value out of your subscription, you're a delusional glazer that needs to rethink life. Their 5-10 servers a day doesn't cost thousands of people's subscriptions, neither does the copy older other game's content ideas you cannot ever manifest in your own thoughts. There's so much more out there than us destroying our sanity over these out of touch, investor catering, ball and chain developers. Investors only know greed and power trips. They have no idea what a game should really be about. Never will. If a game is backed by investors, just move on. Nothing to see here but your wallet and sanity depleting. Hopefully they finally do what is right, but I have little to no faith in this company. Don't let your dreams become memes.

I would not recommend this game unless you can get it for 5-10$ like I did and only care about seeing the story. There is some enjoyable content when you first start, as some of the questlines were interesting enough, but after 20-30 hours you have done all the questlines, all the dailies, the broken expeditions and the singular raid. Once you've completed that 20ish hours of content, what comes next? You might be thinking many single player games are only 20-30 hours and still worth experiencing, but this is meant to be a forever service game that keeps you playing while doing almost nothing to ensure you do so. So you start running the daily ops (which was broken for over 6 months, only recently "fixed") and get no reward, run the same raid and it's like 3-4 variations over and over, do the same 15ish world events every 20 minutes for one of the myriad premium currencies, or grind the season pass where approximately half the rewards are tied to their monthly subscription or just not interesting (this seasons theme is broken bathroom items and the poorly implemented fishing minigame.) Those Daily Ops are still reportedly broken for many, so half a year wait for that went nowhere; you can't complete 2 of the 5 expeditions right now due to being broken for just as long as the Daily Ops, so that's a sizable chunk of the end game content that's just not even functional atm. Many world events are broken like Eviction notice, where you spend 10 minutes standing there shooting maybe 20 total super mutants due to their spawns being broken, and the few that do spawn immediately get spawn camped before the games ♥♥♥♥♥♥ web infrastructure can make them legendary.

They however have "fixed" many things that just lead to a worse gameplay and QOL experience all around as god forbid you have fun or find something useful in the world spawns. You will chase a meta around new mods just for them to utterly nerf them into the ground because they still can't figure out how to organically make their game challenging. You have to engage in ridiculous trading markets with fake currencies to get around their iron grip on how much money you can even earn every 24 hours. It's just not fun to play, every update seems to make it worse, they introduce questlines that they abandon for years and never return to like the last half of Skyline valley, but they find new inventive ways to monetize it and rip you off in either time or money. 

This game rightfully earned it's reputation and I don't need to tell people of how it launched, but let my hours in the game show that even a fallout superfan mod maker can't find the fun in this one. I withheld making this review for 3 major updates, hoping they would finally begin to address my many complaints, issues, and concerns, or to tease something truly interesting, and it just doesn't happen. After seeing this latest season pass, the fact they haven't updated most of the things that have been broken for half a year, and the fact that the brand new infestations are functionally unplayable without hosting your own private server, I can confidently say that I don't think there is a positive future on the horizon for this game.

it has improved GREATLY since release, still a little buggy here and there and doesnt run perfect but thats just the nature of a multiplayer version of fallout 4 effectivly with even more added on like events and other stuff

Used to be on an upward trajectory, everything seamed like it was getting good; combat, the world and other stuff. Now its ♥♥♥♥ and hardly works , just a cash grab really. Dissapointing as I have personally spent over $500 on the game.
This game is full of bugs and sucks because of that. Bethesda is just effing lazy in not fixing it. WFT> This couldve been a decent game.
It is supposed to be multiplayer, but you can not finish quests with other players. I wanted to play this game with my wife, but it is not fun, when you must do everything separately.

this is by far the worst in the fallout series i am utterly flabbergasted at how bad this is! its not stable the content is a joke the updates and new equipment is just reskinning the quest are drawn out and rewards are a insulting Bethesda took my favorite IP and just treated it like trash turned it in to a horrible an embarrassing cash grab showing zero respect to the fan base i would call this a joke but nothing about it is funny
Atomic shop prices seem almost predatory.  Restricting inventory makes the game nearly unplayable without monthly membership fees.

New mechanic is awful and of course per usual the devs try to railroad you into interacting with it using seasonal challenges. The new assaultron bosses do more damage than the EN06 guardian, which is a raid boss. People quit doing raids because of the difficulty and frustration compared to its rewards and apparently the response was to make a tougher enemy and plop them down in the open world. Another fumble that comes as no surprise from the studio that updates fallout 4 periodically just to make the game unstable or break existing mechanics.
The seasons declined very fast this year. The game honestly is in a horrible state. The "new" ohio part of the map was very short and very empty. Might as well play modded fallout 4.

2026. Six years after the release date on Steam. This is a buggy game. No joke. I can not even complete the tutorial "quest" of placing the camp. I gave up and joined a few co-op sessions instead and had a good time, but I quickly lost interest. Still couldn't place down the camp or whatever it asked me to do. I had to ignore it. I should have asked for a refund within the allotted time but I was playing with friends to give it a shot because they like it a lot. Lesson learned I guess...
Only 56% of players have reached lvl 10. That does not require much play time. I let those stats speak for themselves.
The game is fun. I've had some server issues but overall not too bad. If you are a Fallout fan you will likely still enjoy it despite the quirks.

This game is probably what it feels like to steer a helicopter while drunk. 

I've played fallout since the first one and waited for this one to "level out" before starting it. I'm almost at 40 hours and spend most of my time fighting lag or stumbling through the ui. And it still isn't playable with this lag. Why cant this be single player? Connecting online to a game that should be single player is so ridiculous. I'm trying to like this but can't play. Every time I try it hard crashes to shutdown.

This game scratches that Fallout itch I've been feeling, while not being the same-ol' same-ol' you get from replaying games after so many times. I have no doubts in my mind that it'd be a much better experience if you had a friend group in which you played together on a regular basis, but as a solo experience, it's not too difficult.
My only major grievances being the seeming arbitrary nature of acquiring many of the plans for crafting, and the carry-over issue from Fallout 4 of the bullet sponge enemies. (Which is especially frustrating when coming across random level 50 mini-boss enemies as a low-level)

Played for 3 hours, was having 6/10 fun. Some visual and enemy bugs, but playable.
Went to sleep, tried to play the game next day - unable to load the world, game crashes to desktop.

This level of bugs and instability is completely mindboggling in a game 7 years in live-ops. 

And, well, shoved to brim with consumable microtransactions that you need to make the game comfortably playable. 

Cannot recommend.

The game is buggy and broken. Certain things like the power armor bug out and you can't even progress in some quests thanks to the characters being "busy" when they are just standing there looking at you with the stupidest expression on their face refusing to move on to the next part (looking at you Johnny). A game that has been out this long and developed by a goliath of studio like Bethesda and there are still problems like this is just downright embarrassing. Its no wonder that Starfield was a failure. Writing is bad and dull, plus all the characters have dialogue that goes on for far too long and you dread having to talk to anyone. Base building is pointless and has no impact on anything as far as I could tell. But above all that makes this game not good is the fact that it is just boring. Incredibly boring. The only fun aspects of the game are the raids and group events, but you need to already have people with you that have sunk so many hours into the game in order to make it even possible.
cant even play the game because of balckscreen. literally cant even see the main menu but can hear it. just give us fallout 5

The game looks nice and thats only good thing about it. Its full of bugs and the worst thing about it is forced multiplayer feature and no singleplayer option. If you want to play alone, pay 15$ for a monthly subscription. I want to play alone without paying a montly price for it.

you are better off playing this game on a console. the only way to turn off v-sync is to go in the game's files and modify them yourself. even then its no guarantee it'll work. i tried it and couldn't tell if my problem got better or worse. game runs at 60 FPS but the second i move the mouse around the display stutters like i'm watching it through a 1950's film projector. i bought this when i had worse specs and figured it was them. i've since upgraded my PC and these issues still persist. wonder how much i have to pay in microtransactions for todd to get off his giant pile of cash and command his employees to fix these game breaking issues.
Awesome game, 167 hours in and still havent completed main quest line <3 Love all the fallout series!!

You will not find a game with more bugs and glitches than Fallout 76. Game has the potential to be a 9/10. Currently, with the amount of bugs that Bethesda refuses to fix (they have been around for years) I'd rank this game a 4/10.

Worst Fallout game by a large margin.

Probably the worst triple A title I've played iin general.

Low effort updates, devs is holding this game in hostage situation and making this game being their own playground and they don't care about what you would suggest to them, atp i quit, good luck implementing your AI Next-Gen update and promote it as the super big and insane never seen update in whole time planet Earth have ever existed...

played for 3 hours and i am shocked by how absolutely empty this thing is. While i may not like it i can see people who just want a sandbox experience to enjoy it. The ENTIRE playtime i had was spent running around, and while there are a lot of location on the map, I quickly found 4 places that were just horror landscapes. Screaming in the distance, random shots coming from yards away because the cpu saw me first. constantly being ran up on by scorched, it was a weird experience. I feel like because the world is so empty (outside of some starting friendly places) it gave this game a weirdly dark tone which isnt something i was expecting. I waited some time (and a sale) for this because i thought a sandbox was a bit weird, but i read some updates and they said they added NPC's... and by adding NPC's they meant random default skinned people scattered throughout the world with minimum dialogue, usually in the form of asking for things, or asking you to get things.

I ran into an old facility and i got a quest to clear it, so i did. Then i got a quest to claim it, which i thought was cool and so i did. Then i got a popup that other people may want to take it, and i thought that was odd but noone came, so all good. Then, i kid you not, after 1 minute from claiming the facility, i got a notice to defend it...... wait what? im supposed to babysit this thing? Nah, so i left and let it get taken. 

I dont know who wants to spend dozens upon dozens of hours playing this where the main goal is just... accumulate. Go here, find this, build this so you can go there, to find this, to build that, to go elsewhere. Yeah im good, if i wanted to play something with a world this empty with nothing but enemies in the overworld, i would go play a mmorpg. At least their npc's actually have something to say.

Ahhh.. yes. 76. We were off to a bad start back in 2018. A ♥♥♥♥♥♥ first date, if you will. Then, around 2020 something shifted and I was back to give it another try - that was a big comeback. I loved it ever since. While the game had its ups and downs, I think it's fun (that's what it's all about, right?) and in a solid place these days. Seems like the devs are cooking more stuff, so I guess I'm in. Give it a try, but beware - Fallout 1st monthly membership is somewhat important for the overall experience (crafting materials storage, etc.), but totally up to you on how you wanna roll.

Worst game to ever call its self fallout complete trash i dont need a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ team to play ♥♥♥♥ this ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ never has it ever been so hard to go to a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ vault this is just a money hole and ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ scam
There's good and bad to this game. If you value your playtime and money - stay away.

At first its a whole new world with a new world to explore..... then you realise its nothing but doing the same public event challenges. Story is boring and repetitive and Junk is unless if you dont plan to build a settlement. Can't sell bullets so any you collect will pile up in storage with useless junk. Want more storage? Cough up Real money otherwise just throw away stuff and only collect junk to repair your weapons and ammo that you truly need. Gameplay is nice, pretty much the same in fallout 4.

If you dont have friends or no friends that play Fallout 76 with then you might as well just play Fallout 4. Fallout 4 you can do much more and mod it to your own liking. I say again, There is no point to play Fallout 76 by yourself if you already have Fallout 4. With Friends it is fun at times but again after a bit its just the same public challenges.

Overall not a bad game ive been into it a lot as of right now the reasons im giving it a thumbs down is because of how annoying the crashes are and how the game is hard to progress in main story parts by not letting me move at all when im freely able to and when joining public teams it just does not let me at times also along with fallout first i understand the idea but 14 bucks a month is a little much for a game that most people paid full price for. Now the game itself is fun i do enjoy it just those problems make me want to just quit a lot of the time playing it.

Got a Fallout 1st membership but it didnt register in game, after back and forth with Bethesda customer help I was told I have to wait 3 days before it kicks in and if it dosent then I can go through the process all over again. So I paid for something for one month but have to wait for 3 days before I can use it for less time then the month that I paid for. I wont be doing Fallout 1st again.

loved it when i first started playing, met new friends got great weapons had fun. now in the last year or so the devs have nerfed almost every weapon, armor and build style in the and call it balanced. it isn't balancing when you need to rebuild your player cards every update. i think the biggest problem with this game now is the servers, always killing off npc because they cant handle the resources. same amount of players but the servers are so overloaded they free up memory by killing npc so one cant get things like treasure hunters. i just watched two drop over before i could get to them.

This has been a long time coming, grappling with my take on 76 but after 4k hours on ps4 and almost 900 on pc I think I am finally ready to give my 2 cents on it. 

Another nothing season on it's way, adding essentially nothing useful to the game, nothing that would entice long time players to consider coming back or paying for the sub. Usually I can skip one crappy season and the next will be alright, but now it is just a series of mid seasons that I have no choice to skip. The gameplay loop remains unshaken, there are still no cross server raid queues (which means you either server hop for an hour to find a group that will leave or go to the discord to have some chud tell you to run their mediocre build or you cant join them), daily Ops and expeditions have been pushed completely to the wayside and give nothing worthwhile, the public event system is just awful since a vast majority of public events give nothing useful (most of us have all the legendary crafting mats we need from scrapping t3s and t4s), we also have only have had only one decent weapon added in how many years now? Everything else has garbage leg effects that you cannot change or cannot have leg effects at all (honestly the cosmic knife having no leg effects but tons of mods felt like a punch to the groin).

You guys have not shaken the gameplay up in years, raids are impossible to find a group for, the AC expeditions doesn't have anyone queuing (not that they ever did in the first place), and to top it all off YOU REMOVED CONTENT (Milepost Zero was axed instead of fixing Brahmin pathing just so new players coming from the show wouldn't think the game is a buggy mess when it most certainly is).  Things like the keycard bug still softlocks players, especially new players, there are still soft locks present in the BoS questline, the Settler questline, and the AC questline. When does this stuff get fixed guys? Why keep adding more useless garbage when you could clearly make 76 better by fixing existing bugs, adding useful rewards to existing content, and adding cross server queues for daily ops, expeditions, and raids? 

I have to accept that Bethesda only cares about nickle and diming what is left of the player base with seasons (They lowered the cost of buying levels on the season pass just to entice people to p2w the pass and moved from the colorful scoreboards every season to a soulless battlepass page) and atom shop (during the fishing update floating platforms for a pier/dock were put in the cash shop not the season pass). I was addicted to this game, seeing the season pass go up, seeing my build slowly become perfected, and only this last season did I realize how absolutely stupid I was for being so invested in 76. There is no end game, there is nothing to do after you make a good build except for making more builds or hunting down useless display items. Hell you can't even hunt for rare items anymore because Bethesda will inevitably make them useless and will inevitably turn them into a common drop.

Then there was how raids were handled, there are tons of issues with the raid, hell there is one bug where Epsilon squad will be outside of their tanks in their boss room before you even start and they'll just nuke you. Don't worry though, Bethesda has constantly targeted the real issue with the raid, that's right, they took care of people solo farming the first boss that already had low tier drops by lowering the drop rates, removing certain drops, nerfing the boss damage so ricochet wouldn't take his shield down quickly, and nerfing an entire slew of weapons just because they killed the boss quickly. They constantly, since the raid was added, and even to this day, target solo farmers in the raid acting like it is the most pressing issue in all of 76, real glad they can nerf solo farming for mid drops instead of fixing that Brahmin pathfinding, gotta tackle those actual problems am i right? 

The Ghoul update was handled poorly. Yes, please boo me, it was and still is ass. You need a disguise to enter the base of two factions (raider and settler) because they "don't trust ghouls" yet there are ghouls INSIDE OF THOSE AREAS ALREADY. Not to mention there is a fuggin super mutant inside the raider faction hq and even with the disguise both factions still recognize that it is you. It makes a ton of sense for the BoS or the SS, I understand those, but the raiders and settlers? It wouldn't even be that big of an issue if the gold vendor for the raiders was not inside their hq, but he is. It's safe to say though that stuff like this will never be fixed by Bethesda in any meaningful way though, they gotta keep knocking down those raid farmers. 

Fishing was done poorly. In every other MMO there is a use for fishing in some way shape or form, there is a story, or fish are used in high end recipes that payers can sell, there is some kind of use for it. In 76 there is no use. Your fishing dailies give upgrades for the fishing rod, the chum you can make only really gives you 2 star legs which is worthless especially when a majority of the player base already has most leg crafting unlocked. On top of that all the crafting recipes for fish are either worthless or there are better cheaper/easily accessible alternatives. That leaves fishing as pretty much just a cosmetic thing with displaying fish on mounts.

The AC update was a travesty. The new expeditions blow, they are ok for farming solo but honestly? All AC really added was a massive dead empty worldspace with absolutely nothing to do. There are 1-2 short af side missions there and then a main story that really doesn't have you doing much in AC but talking to people. Sure it added civil engineer armor but I would argue that SS armor is still the best armor available in game and honestly doesn't require half as much grinding as the CE armor. The same goes for the Pitt PA, there are better cheaper alternatives so why grind for it at all? The AA use to be the only reason to farm expeditions but it was nerfed so hard to stop solo farmers that there is no point in ever trying to use it again especially since now a vast majority of melee weapons, especially unarmed melee, do significantly more damage than it now. 

76 use to be incredible, the BoS expansion was episodic, added new weapons like the War Glaive, and was and still is fun to play through. The settlers expansion added two big factions, new hub areas, new weapons, new armor, and were just all around great additions to the world. The problem is that they have not kept up with that quality over the years. Even when we get a large worldspace like Skyline Valley or the more recent Rust Kingdom there really isn't much there. The quest lines are incredibly short, filled with "go here and talk to this guy, then go there and talk to this guy" and just feel like padding to make the game feel more fleshed out than it really is. I'll concede that the boss fight at the end of Skyline Valley was fun, but everything else was incredibly boring. The most they really add is just new public events which do have decent rewards, bounty hunting alone can net you tons of mats but again in a game where everyone already has tons of those crafting mats, most leg crafting unlocked, and have finished all of their builds, what is the point? 

It is clear at this point that the quality is just going to continue to decline, the community grow smore fanatical with each season, bashing anyone who doesn't enjoy an update, so Bethesda has no real reason to provide a higher standard of content. The community will keep paying for subs, keep bashing others for speaking out, and all Bethesda has to do is give them another big dead empty worldspace to wander around in every year or two and they will be content. 

I have more to say but I think I have given 76 far more time than I ever should have.

300 hours into the game and i must say, 6 years after release bugs should be ironed out or atleast the most game breaking bugs gone, that is not the case, main quests can get stuck where there is no way you can ever complete that quest, if the mob spawned below the floor or inside a wall and you cant hit it, that quest will never be able to complete and your stuck there, Support staff dont even do anything about missing Atoms brought in the store weeks after payment has been made.

Early Access game and support that dont care about you as a customer, if you wanted to play Fallout online just dont.

LMAO FO76 has been a joke since the beginning. This game has been their money mine on the Fallout fanbase ever since they brought in NPCs. They aren't going to fix anything. Empty words of "apology" in hopes it'll keep yall spending money on this atrocious game. The ONLY reason I played this game was because I needed that "Fallout" scratch when the show came out. L Bethesda. You aren't getting a DIME from me. If you're still paying into this game or even playing the game at all, you are apart of the problem. If you really want to see a difference, Quit the game and hurt their pockets. let server lvls die, and let their monetary income they're getting off us D I E. literally the only way you can make them even consider listening to what the community wants.

I was introduced to 76 on console back during the Nuka-World season. Assumed things I paid for would carry over to the pc version, like most other "RPG"s. I was wrong. 

I'd be okay with starting over if not for the fact that every recent update has been a step back towards the buggy mess that gave 76 its almost unshakeable bad reputation and most of the things required to make a build useable or fun to play are locked behind content new players can't even finish, with drop rates below 1% at times, and bullet sponges that are miserable to fight with small teams. It takes thousands of attempts and scrapping duplicate weapons just to learn how to make it reload a little faster.

Trying to enter power armor has gotten you locked in place for the last half a decade, weapons and perks don't work as intended or get "fixed" into an unusable state just to force the same few meta builds into prevalence, and for some reason the game somehow runs worse on new devices than it does on a crusty 7 year old Playstation 4. 

I love the worldbuilding and faithfulness to folklore from the state it takes place in, having grown up there, but it just feels so unfun and tedious to play now.

Ever since staff were replaced by former EA employees, each season has been more of a push to buy a subscription than the last. You used to get cool rewards from daily challenges, now you need to subscribe to Bethesda to earn more than a painted toilet to build in your camp. I'm not joking
After the main story, side quests, even with seasonal events, it gets monotonous. You basically run out of excuses to continue playing. It's like new game plus, but without the replay value.

I have sunk a few hundred hours into this game, so I clearly like it, but I think this is a season where I'll sit it out. It's a bad update. Infestations were poorly thought out and too annoying to actually put effort into. They're the main schtick this season and are basically a worse version of headhunts that aren't worth your time. If the next season is better or they rework this one, I'll update my review, but as for now, byeeeeeeeeeeeee~.
There is no chat system, they literally force you to use a mic.  Not everyone wants to or can use a mic, esp. those of us with families.  Seemed like it could have been a decent game.

I've had an amusement park uniform in my inventory since May of 2022 due to Bethesda's refusal to fix the Lucky Mucker quest bug. I've completed the entire game with it in my pocket. I'm tired of carrying this dress around.

40$ for a game with bugs that will never get fixed, despite being known since the game came out 6 years ago.

Finally the pvp update we've all been waiting for!!! (unless you have 1st that is), Infestations in current state suck to the point of being pvp to get there first and kill the mobs within seconds. Atleast with bounty hunts they have the timer before they start. AND ontop of the trash infestations which also has Dailies/Weeklies tied to it, ontop of all that the season rewards suck for the most part. Fallout 76 quickly became my most played Fallout game in the series and this update just aint it, the rest of the game has come aways from release but this update is just sour to me

I recommend trying it for new and returning Fallout fans as, despite its poor launch and the dreadful state of the game in its first 3 years, I'd say that it has gradually improved and the loyal fans that stayed did get treated well enough. I honestly believe that the continuous support for the game will get it far and it will sure deliver good content the more support it gets. Try it out!
Ive always been a big fan of the Fallout series and consider this a fantastic addition. Still plenty of kinks to workout, but its come a long way!

To be honest, I've never played a game that made me turn my back on an entire series.... until 76 and ESO.  These two games were such a reversal of what to expect that I couldn't play their betters anymore.  After trying again and again and again to play these two games, then trying to go back with a game like Starfield, New Vegas,  Skyrim or the Obli remake.... I just.... can't.  I never thought that this game would break my desire to play a bethesda game, but it did, so I guess... thanks Bethesda for introducing me to a much wider world of gaming.

Movement keys stop responding constantly along with getting stuck in a blank pipboy and unable to play without restarting, and sometimes that doesnt even work.

not to mention, this game is seriously laggy for 2026.

Doesn't load right half the time when trying to boot up the game, feels like you need the subscription just to play the game cause of how much they overload your inventory with loot that you don't want to just scrap or drop but then end up having to cause of the arbitrary weight limits and stash box limits not to mention display items still count to that weight and so if you want a cool camp with all your items out you have to fill your stash with junk and then that leaves you with jack for storage when you want to craft stuff or cook or sell things. it gets to a point. we bought the game yet can't play it cause of greed needed to sustain a parasite.
It's Fallout, with lots of radiation, NPCs, and other Fallout things. How many Fallout things? Yes. Should you play it? Fallout.

I wish they'd fix server problems over constantly tweaking the UI and mechanics, but again, it's fun and if you go in with the right expectations, it's worth it.  I don't have Fallout 1st and don't plan on it.  The game is totally playable without it.  Overall I'd give it a 7/10.

its a very good game for a certain type of person that enjoys this type of game also never watch a build guide on the game just make ur own that’s what makes it fun.

if you have played a fallout game before, don't buy this game. this game is not for you. this was my first fallout game, and when i played it for the first time, it was the best game i have ever played. when i played it, it was great. i loved everything about it, but when i played fallout 4 after this game, fallout 76 just suddenly felt like a really bad game. fallout 76 does not live up to the fallout series. it is a game that is strictly multiplayer unless you have a subscription. the story is very hard to follow, and it is filled with in game transactions. because it is strictly multiplayer, they had to sacrifice a lot of things which made fallout so good. i love this game, but compared to other fallout games, it's not good. the game is good though, would give it a 7 or 8 out of 10.

Its the worst Fallout game there is. But still its fallout so Im playing it...

The power armour is goated though!

After playing this game for 1600h i can recommend this game to you. 

You will be finding out stuff and doing quests until you are around level 1000.

But after that you are going to go through the motions everytime you start the game, but you will still start the game and do some dailyes and check what atom shop has for free and what season items are. and check where minerva is. 

The seasons arent bad, but they arent great. it will give you something to do for a hot minute after 5 other level 5k players decimate the thing that is happening, but you get rewards so thats nice. 

What is great about this game though is the exploration you do when you are a new player, or up too level 1000ish. you will find some hidden bobbleheads here and there, mabye even a rare recepie you have no idea is rare until you have thrown it away for the fifth time and you see players sell em for 3000+ caps. same with some weapons. looking at you enclave. 

The long quest lines are in my opinion the best, they make you go around all over doing long quest lines and looking for clues, but suddenly the quest log is emty and you dont find any more, so after that the game dies down. but then you have done alot of hours in the game and i think that is the value in it self.

The raid is also a good one, but i feel that would be better with a ingame chat or atleast party chat. but it is buggy and there is most of the time one in the group who is walking trough doors taking you to the end boss, or spamming the first boss. so again atleast you will get rewards.

objectively this games like a high 6 to a low 7. However me personally this is a really fun fallout themed timekiller with a enough interesting stuff to see and do to keep you invested, beats the hell out of fallout 4
very good game, come along way since the beginning. Can seem overwhelming to start with but remove the side quests and concentrate on main quest and you will soon get the hang of it.

76 hours on 76 its a great game but i feel like the biggest issue about it is just the blatant amount of microtransactions and the lack of story in this game yes there is a story but it isnt a great story the two best parts about this game is the building (which has most of the stuff stuck behind microtransactions) and the multiplayer which is kind of weirdly set up I also feel that power armor sometimes is just too easy to get compared to any other fallout including fallout 4 but overrall its pretty good game

It should be seperate so you can have room for more. Other than that I love the game!

I LOVE this game. It's the post apocolypic world I want to live in, more Walking Dead and less Mad Max. I am not a gamer but as New Englander (Boston) my son made me learn the play FO4. FO4 was the first thing I played since my SEGA CD in the late 90's, lol. I LOVED FO4 but I really like the community of FO76. I took a year break in 2025 and the added content has been great. Much more enjoyable. Don't listen to all the hater's.

Huge game to explore, took me months to get to level 1000 and explore all location. Seems like bethesda has a good short term plan for expansion of the game as well.

i have played all fallout games in the era of their release. fallout 76 i have put the most hours into. it just keeps me going. i did not play f76 at release as there was much controversy around it and i was fine with fallout 4. then in feb 2025 i tried it, and love it.

The game has flaws (all FO/ES games do), diehard FO fans may have their issues, new players may have the same issues... Bottom line, don't think to hard, enjoy the story and lore of the world. Its a pretty good game over all.
This game is still so immersive even after years of being out. Theres always something to find or do

My boyfriend and I bought Fallout 76 while it was on sale for ridiculously cheap (around £6 from memory). I’d previously avoided F76 like the plague due to all the well‑deserved criticism it got back in the day when it was an empty, buggy game.

It has been the best £6 I’ve ever spent, and I’m annoyed I didn’t get this game sooner. I was officially hooked a couple of hours in when a Protectron called “Insult Bot” spawned directly in front of me (scaring the ♥♥♥♥ out of me), then proceeded to roast me for eating mouldy food earlier in the game, and said that if “he wanted to throw up, he would’ve looked at my face.”

If you love the lore behind Fallout, this game has a rich amount to uncover, which keeps it interesting and immersive. Also, the map is absolutely HUGE. I’ve spent almost 90 hours in the game and I’ve still got so much more to explore. I loved the gameplay of Fallout 4 (which this game uses the same mechanics for), but that game felt empty lore‑wise and immersion‑wise, whereas F76 has both, in my opinion.

The map is so extensive and you can really do whatever the hell you want, but there are plenty of quests, including a main storyline, to give you structure. I love the different areas and biomes, my favourite being Cranberry Bog - I feel like everything is so interesting to look at in this game.

There have been so many occasions where this game has borderline given me a heart attack, especially at a lower level. Enemies are more difficult to defeat than in the mainline games, and there’s no pausing in VATS or your Pipboy when you’ve got a Legendary Deathclaw running up to you. One time I entered some kind of Protectron station, passed by a bunch of what I thought were dismembered Protectrons, and suddenly around 20 Protectrons rose from the ground behind me and started flanking me. 
Another time I spotted the "Mothman" from a long distance away with a rifle, so I took a shot, thinking I was safe -  barely made a dent. Actual bricks were shat as he immediately flies up into the air and lands directly in front of me. No pausing, no fast travel,  just screaming, panicking, and spamming stimpaks as I tried to survive LOL.

Some of the new enemies in-game - The Scorched, the Lost - are genuinely creepy and ominious, with the way they look and the sounds they make, which I like, as I'd gotten used to ghouls and super mutants.

My boyfriend and I couldn’t really get into the multiplayer aspect since it doesn’t work well with story quests, so I mainly play by myself on a private server.

The “microtransactions” don’t feel that deep to me. I’ve got Fallout First with no issues, unlike a lot of other reviewers. The Atomic Shop is mainly centred around building your camp, so you don’t have to buy any of that to enjoy the game.

It reached 5/5 stars when I could adopt a cat that brings me legendary weapons. I’ve got a little tabby who just rocks up to me and drops a legendary Fat Man into my inventory. Sick.

90 hours in and this game hasn't gotten boring to me at all. I really look forward to playing it after I finish work.
really stepped up after the launch disaster. fun to play now many story lines. many events. updates very often and all free

negative side is for game 8 years old youd think it would be more stable somewhat, has its features, great fun lvling in team with friends and loads of quests and base building

It's just something that I can kill my time on. Good with friends too. Would recommend

Absolute worst of both worlds. The instanced questing makes your few choices have zero impact on anything, while forcing parties to play separately to progress. Had a group of friends excited to try it and they all gave up after the first day of walking together to do solo content.

I’ve played too much of this game throughout its development and can safely say anything you can do here with your friends or randoms is a watered down version of the quality you’d get from Bethesdas other Fallout games. Fallout 76 is a great experience in the beginning but slowly but surely devolves into a monotonous and boring grind and build up to nothing …..there’s nothing to work towards in this game besides collecting nonsense. And might I mention that nonsense they sell on the Atom store is all overpriced garbage that serves only to make you forget how ♥♥♥♥ this game really is under the hood. Also I’d like to emphasize how bad the Multiplayer (main focus of this dumpster fire game) isn’t even really built around playing with your friends consistently. Instead it turns into whatever seems the least boring to do. 1/10 from random game informant. 

I really wish I could say there’s something of value here to redeem the game but I genuinely hope they close this chapter out and actually gives us news on Elder scrolls 6 .

Better than Described!  Fits the bill for a classic Fallout game!  You can group up for added benefits and choose to not PVP which I like being relatively new.  Has some of the building concepts from FA4 if you like that (and who doesn't?)  Worst part is trying to communicate with other players.  You need to know them away from the game and Discord to make it easier.  Communication is really the big loss in this as a MMORPG.

The game as of right now has come a long way. Bug fixes, patches, expansions, events etc. All that has truly transformed the game into something worthwhile. If you liked Fallout 4, you will like this game. If you're more of a NV or 3 fan you might not. Its still a strong Fallout title as of today. It has a bunch of hours of content and for the love of god stop calling it an MMO. Its just not... I played the whole game solo and never needed anyone. Sure some events are cool and other players help, but the game as a whole can me played solo without any issues or obstacles. DO NOT buy Fallout 1st as it is just a greedy cash-grab tactic. Overall I have not encountered any game breaking bugs or glitches so I am giving this game a 7.5/10. It is worth to buy it while its on Sale and since it happens often I would recommend to wait for one before buying.
